ddls:
CREATE TABLE "actors" (
  "actor_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each actor */,
  "actor_name" TEXT /* Full name of the actor */,
  "total_gross" REAL /* Total gross earnings of the actor */,
  "number_of_movies" INTEGER /* Number of movies the actor has been a part of */,
  "average_per_movie" REAL /* Average earnings per movie for the actor */,
  "actor_birthdate" TEXT /* Birthdate of the actor */,
  "actor_gender" TEXT /* Gender of the actor */,
  "actor_nationality" TEXT /* Nationality of the actor */,
  "actor_bio" TEXT /* Brief biography of the actor */,
  "actor_popularity_score" INTEGER /* Popularity score based on social media and other metrics */,
  "actor_awards" TEXT /* Notable awards received by the actor */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("actor_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "movies" (
  "movie_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each movie */,
  "movie_name" TEXT /* Title of the movie */,
  "gross_earnings" REAL /* Gross earnings of the movie */,
  "release_year" INTEGER /* Release year of the movie */,
  "studio" TEXT /* Studio that produced the movie */,
  "movie_genre" TEXT /* Genre of the movie */,
  "movie_director" TEXT /* Name of the director */,
  "movie_producer" TEXT /* Name of the producer */,
  "movie_runtime" INTEGER /* Runtime of the movie in minutes */,
  "movie_rating" REAL /* Average rating of the movie */,
  "movie_synopsis" TEXT /* Brief synopsis of the movie */,
  "movie_budget" REAL /* Budget of the movie */,
  "movie_country" TEXT /* Country of production */,
  "movie_distributor" TEXT /* Name of the distributor */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("movie_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_movies_studio FOREIGN KEY ("studio") REFERENCES studios ("studio_name")
)

CREATE TABLE "actor_movie" (
  "actor_id" INTEGER /* ID of the actor */,
  "movie_id" INTEGER /* ID of the movie */,
  "character_name" TEXT /* Name of the character played by the actor */,
  "actor_role" TEXT /* Role of the actor (e.g., lead, supporting) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("actor_id", "movie_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "studios" (
  "studio_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each studio */,
  "studio_name" TEXT /* Name of the studio */,
  "studio_founder" TEXT /* Name of the founder of the studio */,
  "studio_foundation_year" INTEGER /* Year the studio was founded */,
  "studio_headquarters" TEXT /* Location of the studio's headquarters */,
  "studio_description" TEXT /* Brief description of the studio */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("studio_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "awards" (
  "award_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each award */,
  "award_name" TEXT /* Name of the award */,
  "award_category" TEXT /* Category of the award */,
  "award_year" INTEGER /* Year the award was given */,
  "award_description" TEXT /* Brief description of the award */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("award_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "actor_awards" (
  "actor_id" INTEGER /* ID of the actor */,
  "award_id" INTEGER /* ID of the award */,
  "movie_id" INTEGER /* ID of the movie for which the award was received */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("actor_id", "award_id", "movie_id")
)
column_names: Total gross earnings of the actor
Number of movies the actor has been a part of
Average earnings per movie for the actor
Popularity score based on social media and other metrics
Notable awards received by the actor
Runtime of the movie in minutes
Name of the character played by the actor
Role of the actor (e.g., lead, supporting)
Name of the founder of the studio
ID of the movie for which the award was received
